title: HTTP Forge Configuration
description: Configuration file for HTTP Forge VS Code extension

storage.description: Storage configuration for collections and data

format.description: Storage format for collections. 'folder' stores each request as a folder with separate files for meta.json and body. 'json' stores collections as single JSON files.

root.description: Root directory for HTTP Forge data (collections, environments, flows, suites)

history.description: Directory for request history storage (can be gitignored)

results.description: Directory for test suite results storage (can be gitignored)

maxRedirects.description: Maximum number of redirects to follow

tls.description: Default TLS settings for HTTPS requests

caPath.description: Path to CA certificate bundle (PEM), relative to workspace root or absolute

certPath.description: Path to client certificate (PEM), relative to workspace root or absolute

keyPath.description: Path to client private key (PEM), relative to workspace root or absolute

pfxPath.description: Path to client certificate bundle (PFX/P12), relative to workspace root or absolute

certificates.description: Host-based client certificate mappings (Postman-style global certificates)

host.description: Host matcher: exact host (api.example.com), wildcard (*.example.com), or *

port.description: Optional port matcher (defaults to 443 for HTTPS)

caPath.description: Path to CA certificate bundle (PEM), relative to workspace root or absolute

certPath.description: Path to client certificate (PEM), relative to workspace root or absolute

keyPath.description: Path to client private key (PEM), relative to workspace root or absolute

pfxPath.description: Path to client certificate bundle (PFX/P12), relative to workspace root or absolute

modulePaths.description: Paths to search for custom modules in pre/post request scripts. Relative to workspace root.

scope.description: Script scope mode. 'shared' (default): all script levels and the pre/post phases share one JavaScript scope. 'isolated': each script level runs in its own scope (Postman-compatible); state passes through pm.variables/pm.environment/pm.globals.

timeout.description: Per-script timeout in milliseconds. Bounds both the synchronous script CPU guard and the async event-loop drain budget (pending setTimeout / un-awaited Promises) the runner waits on before advancing to the next request.

runner.description: Collection runner and test suite settings

resultsRetentionDays.description: Number of days to retain test results

indexPageSize.description: Number of entries per index page

recentErrorsLimit.description: Maximum number of recent errors to track

default.description: Name of the default environment to use

restClientExport.description: Preferences for exporting collections to REST‑Client format.

path.description: Output path for exported REST‑Client files.  If relative it is resolved against the workspace root.

mergeGlobals.description: Merge global environment variables into each exported environment file.

proxy.description: Proxy configuration (null to disable). Requests are tunnelled through the proxy using native Node.js http/https — no extra packages required.

enabled.description: Toggle the proxy on or off without removing the configuration. Defaults to true when the proxy object is present.

http.description: Full URL of the HTTP proxy, e.g. "http://proxy.corp.com:8080". Credentials can be embedded: "http://user:pass@proxy.corp.com:8080".

https.description: Full URL of the HTTPS proxy (used for HTTPS targets). Falls back to "http" if omitted.

bypass.description: Host patterns to skip the proxy. Supports exact match ("api.local"), wildcard subdomain ("*.corp.com"), or catch-all ("*").
